name: pandadoc
description: List, create, and send PandaDoc documents and track their e-signature status

envVars.name: PANDADOC_API_KEY
envVars.description: PandaDoc API key (sandbox or production), generated on the Configuration page of the Developer Dashboard

tools.name: List Documents
tools.description: List and search documents with optional filtering by status, template, folder, or date

q.description: Search documents by name or reference number

status.description: Filter by status code: 0 draft, 1 sent, 2 completed, 5 viewed, 6 waiting approval, 10 paid, 11 voided, 12 declined

template_id.description: Only return documents created from this template

folder_uuid.description: Only return documents stored in this folder

created_from.description: Only documents created on or after this ISO 8601 timestamp

created_to.description: Only documents created before this ISO 8601 timestamp

historicalSummary.omitted: document version and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Document Status
tools.description: Get basic info and current status of a document — useful to confirm it reached the expected state (e.g. document.draft before sending, document.completed before downloading)

tools.name: Get Document Details
tools.description: Get full details of a document: recipients and their completion status, fields, tokens, pricing tables, metadata, and timestamps

tools.name: List Templates
tools.description: List templates in the workspace to find template IDs for creating documents

folder_uuid.description: Only return templates stored in this folder

historicalSummary.omitted: content placeholders and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Template Details
tools.description: Get a template's roles, tokens, fields, and pricing tables to know what Create Document from Template needs

tools.name: Create Document Link
tools.description: Create a signing session link for a recipient to view and sign a sent document, without sending an email

recipient.description: Email address of the document recipient the session is created for

lifetime.description: How long the link stays valid, in seconds (defaults to 3600 = 1 hour)

tools.name: Create Document from Template
tools.description: Create a new draft document from an existing template, pre-filling recipients, tokens, and fields

template_uuid.description: ID of the template to use (copy it from the template URL in the PandaDoc app)

recipients.description: Recipients the document will be sent to; each entry needs an email (or phone) and may set first_name, last_name, and role matching a template role

tokens.description: Values for template variables, each entry { name, value }

fields.description: Pre-fill template fields: maps field names to { value } objects

metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value data to associate with the document

tags.description: Tags to mark the document with

folder_uuid.description: ID of the folder where the created document should be stored

tools.name: Send Document
tools.description: Send a draft document to its recipients for completion and signing (document must be in document.draft status)

subject.description: Email subject for the notification sent to recipients

message.description: Message included in the email with the link to the document

silent.description: If true, disables email notifications to recipients (useful when sharing the document link another way)

prompts.title: Awaiting signature
prompts.prompt: List my PandaDoc documents that have been sent or viewed but not yet completed, and who still needs to sign.

prompts.title: Create and send a document
prompts.prompt: Create a PandaDoc document from a template I specify, fill in the recipient details, and send it for signature once it reaches draft status.

prompts.title: Check document status
prompts.prompt: Check the status of a PandaDoc document I specify and summarize each recipient's progress.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: PandaDoc API Setup

steps.title: Get a PandaDoc account with API access
steps.description: API access is included with Enterprise plans (active or in trial) and is also sold as standalone API plans — including a free API key for development/sandbox use and paid API Developer plans at https://www.pandadoc.com/api/pricing/. Sandbox keys work during a trial.

steps.title: Generate an API key
steps.description: Open the Developer Dashboard at https://app.pandadoc.com/a/#/api-dashboard/configuration and generate a Sandbox key (for testing) or a Production key (requires approval from PandaDoc).

steps.title: Set the environment variable
steps.description: Add PANDADOC_API_KEY to your .env. Requests authenticate with the header 'Authorization: API-Key <your-key>'.

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run List Documents. To exercise the full flow, create a template in the PandaDoc app, then create a document from it and send it.

notes: API keys are tied to the user who generates them — keys inherit that user's role and license, and are deactivated if the user is removed from the workspace.
After creating a document it stays in document.uploaded for a few seconds before moving to document.draft; poll Get Document Status before calling Send Document.
Sandbox keys have lower rate limits than production keys.
